commentary:
# Cross-Exchange Liquidity Commentary – 2026-07-16

The ranking reveals a pronounced two-tier structure dominated by US equities. Nasdaq 100 and NYSE lead decisively, with ILLIQ costs below 0.0057% per $1M traded and comparable volumes exceeding $206.7M, though Nasdaq's wider 0.7648% AR spread reflects higher tick fragmentation. The mid-tier cluster—Tokyo Stock Exchange, Euronext, Hong Kong Stock Exchange, and SIX Swiss Exchange—shows materially higher price impact (ILLIQ ranging 0.0284% to 0.0364% per $1M), yet tighter spreads in some cases; notably, Euronext's 0.6024% spread rivals NYSE despite weaker turnover at 0.4238%. Korea Exchange and Xetra occupy a secondary tier with elevated ILLIQ costs (0.0496% and 0.1334% per $1M respectively) and compressed volumes under $21M. London Stock Exchange trails at 0.0726% per $1M ILLIQ and 0.7796% spread, making it the least efficient venue. The Korea Exchange exemplifies metric divergence: despite a reasonable 1.4090% spread and 0.8390% turnover, its 0.1334% per $1M ILLIQ cost ranks worst, suggesting price discovery challenges or inventory effects rather than spreads alone.
